description: We are the department for economic growth. We support businesses to invest, grow and export, creating jobs and opportunities across the country. DBT is a ministerial department, supported by 20 agencies and public bodies .

details.body:
We are the department for economic growth. We support businesses to invest, grow and export, creating jobs and opportunities across the country.

DBT is a ministerial department, supported by 20 agencies and public bodies.

details.body: The Interim Permanent Secretary is responsible for leading the Department for Business and Trade, and is the Secretary of State’s principal policy adviser.

role_appointments.title: Amanda Brooks CBE - Interim Permanent Secretary

details.body:
The Director General, Trade Negotiations leads the Department for Business and Trade’s (DBT) negotiating teams in securing free trade agreements (FTAs). These aim to meet the government’s ambition for the UK’s future domestic and global prosperity.

Responsibilities include:

  • agreeing the delivery of an ambitious set of FTAs
  • fulfilling the government’s manifesto pledge to sign agreements with countries accounting for 80% of UK trade

  • delivering a challenging programme of international engagement

role_appointments.title: Amanda Brooks CBE - Director General, Trade Negotiations

details.body:
The Director General, Trade Negotiations leads the Department for International Trade’s (DIT) negotiating teams in securing free trade agreements (FTAs). These aim to meet the government’s ambition for the UK’s future domestic and global prosperity.

Responsibilities include:

  • agreeing the delivering an ambitious set of FTAs
  • fulfilling the government’s manifesto pledge to sign agreements with countries accounting for 80% of UK trade

  • delivering a challenging programme of international engagement

role_appointments.title: Amanda Brooks CBE - Director General, Trade Negotiations

role_appointments.title: Jaee Samant - Director General, Business Group

details.body:
The Director General is responsible for:

  • leading on developing, co-ordinating and delivering the government’s strategy, policy and legislation on crime, policing, fire, international law enforcement and judicial cooperation
  • creating the Emergency Services Network, a 4G digital critical communications network which will transform how Britain’s emergency and rescue services operate, and leading various other national digital law enforcement programmes
  • leading the policy and operational teams working on modern slavery and human trafficking, including the UK’s National Referral Mechanism and provision of support for victims in England and Wales

The DG also serves as a member of the Home Office Executive Committee.

details.body:
The HM Trade Commissioner for Latin America and the Caribbean cooperates closely with the wider diplomatic network and other colleagues to coordinate the government effort overseas to promote UK trade and prosperity.

The Trade Commissioner has full responsibility for all Department for International Trade (DIT) work in Latin America and the Caribbean including:

  • growing the overall bilateral trade and investment relationship
  • improving market access for British companies, including small and medium sized businesses (SMEs)
  • developing trade policy

details.body: The Consul General is the senior UK official in a Consulate General, which is a subordinate office to the Embassy or High Commission, usually located in another major city. The Consul General represents the UK government and is typically responsible for consular, visa and trade activities in their city or region.

details.body:
The Director General for Industrial Strategy is responsible for:

  • overseeing the government’s industrial strategy to provide businesses with the long-term certainty they need to invest in sectors and places of highest growth potential, tackling barriers to increased investment and high-quality jobs, and ensuring tangible impact in communities right across the UK
  • leading the strategic centre of DBT, and the planning and coordination of work to drive sustainable growth, trade and the creation of good jobs, the department’s analysis group, the Chief Economist, the Office for the Chief Scientific Adviser and its global futures directorate

details.body: The Director General sits on the Department for Business and Trade departmental board and executive committee and helps deliver the Secretary of State’s and Minister for Exports’ export priorities.

role_appointments.title: Tim Reid - Director General

role.title: Head of the Business Group, UKEF

role_appointments.title: Tim Reid - Head of the Business Group

details.body:
The UK Export Finance (UKEF) Chief Executive Officer is responsible for management of the department as a whole. As Principal Accounting Officer, the CEO is responsible to ministers and Parliament for the management of UKEF’s operations. UKEF is the UK’s Export Credit Agency, supporting business of all sizes to invest and export overseas. It is a unique ministerial department reporting to the Secretary of State for Business and Trade. The Chief Executive’s responsibilities include:

  • setting the strategic vision for the department
  • facilitating growth of UK exports in line with the government’s export strategy
  • supporting UKEF to provide guarantees to UK exporters to win contracts, fulfil orders and get paid

role_appointments.title: Tim Reid - Interim Chief Executive Officer
Tim Reid - Executive Committee Member

details.body:
Our non-executives are senior figures from outside the department who bring a diverse mix of expertise and skills from across the public and private sector.

They all:

  • give guidance and advice to DBT leaders and ministers
  • support and challenge management on the department’s strategic direction
  • provide support in monitoring and reviewing progress

details.body: The Permanent Secretary is responsible for leading the Department for Business and Trade, and is the Secretary of State’s principal policy adviser.

The Second Permanent Secretary and Chief Trade Negotiation Adviser oversees the trade policy and negotiation agenda for the Department for Business and Trade and supports the Permanent Secretary in running the department.

ordered_roles.title: Second Permanent Secretary and Chief Trade Negotiation Adviser

details.body:
The Director General, Trading Systems is responsible for:

  • bilateral trade policy relations
  • global trade delivery
  • export control and sanctions
  • trade defence
  • global supply chains

The Director General for Exports and UK Trade (DGET) is responsible for driving export growth, delivering jobs and prosperity across the United Kingdom.

The group covers exports, sectors and UK regions and nations. DGET also lead work on the department’s export strategy and programmes, including the Export Campaigns and the Export Academy.

DGET coordinates the department’s contribution to Levelling Up and to strengthening the Union through export growth, as well as ensuring a joined-up and UK-wide approach to trade promotion and investment.

ordered_roles.title: Director General, Exports and UK Trade

details.body: The Office for Investment is a joint unit reporting to the Department for Business and Trade, HM Treasury and Number 10 Downing Street. It works across government to land top-tier investment into the UK.

The Director General, Trade Negotiations leads the Department for Business and Trade’s (DBT) negotiating teams in securing free trade agreements (FTAs). These aim to meet the government’s ambition for the UK’s future domestic and global prosperity.

Responsibilities include:

  • agreeing the delivery of an ambitious set of FTAs
  • fulfilling the government’s manifesto pledge to sign agreements with countries accounting for 80% of UK trade

  • delivering a challenging programme of international engagement

The Director General is responsible for:

  • Brexit opportunities
  • trade, co-operation and the Union
  • trade and investment negotiations for goods and services

The Director General is responsible for:

  • corporate governance
  • consumer and competition
  • labour markets
  • better regulation
  • regulatory delivery

The Director General is responsible for:

  • corporate governance
  • consumer and competition
  • labour markets
  • better regulation
  • regulatory delivery

The Director General is responsible for a number of different priority sectors, including:

  • automotive
  • aerospace
  • manufacturing
  • maritime
  • defence and security
  • steel
  • chemicals
  • critical minerals
  • infrastructure and construction
  • energy
  • energy intensive industries
  • agriculture, food and drink
  • digital, retail, education, creative and sports
  • financial and professional services
  • healthcare and life sciences
  • science and technology
  • consumer sectors
  • Post Office

The Chief Operating Officer has overall responsibility for a range of the Department for Business and Trade’s (DBT) corporate functions and support services, including:

  • digital, data and technology
  • people, place and transformation
  • commercial
  • project delivery and change
  • finance
  • security and business services
  • grants delivery

The HM Trade Commissioner (HMTC) for South Asia oversees all Department for Business and Trade (DBT) work in Bangladesh, India, the Maldives, Nepal, and Sri Lanka:

  • growing bilateral trade and investment relationships
  • improving market access for British companies, including small- and medium-sized businesses (SMEs)
  • developing finance and trade policy

The HMTC also works closely with High Commissioners, Ambassadors, the wider diplomatic network and colleagues across the UK to support the prosperity agenda.

ordered_roles.title: HM Trade Commissioner for South Asia

details.body: The HM Trade Commissioner (HMTC) leads on export promotion, inward and outward investment, and trade policy overseas on behalf of the UK government. Work includes developing and delivering a regional trade plan setting out Department for Business and Trade’s (DBT) priorities in important global markets.

Responsibilities include:

  • advanced manufacturing
  • automotive
  • aerospace
  • sustainability and Infrastructure
  • steel
  • materials
  • maritime and shipbuilding
  • professional and business services
  • Canada and Mexico FTAs
  • economic security
  • sanctions
  • Ukraine reconstruction
  • global supply chains
  • critical minerals
  • trade defence

Responsibilities include:

ordered_roles.title: Minister of State (Minister for International Trade)

details.body:
Responsibilities include:

  • investment strategy
  • investment promotion across all sectors
  • investor relationship management
  • investment events (including Global Investment Summit)
  • DBT Investor Screening
  • Office for Investment
  • defence and security exports

Our non-executives are senior figures from outside the department who bring a diverse mix of expertise and skills from across the public and private sector.

They all:

  • give guidance and advice to DBT leaders and ministers

  • support and challenge management on the department’s strategic direction

  • provide support in monitoring and reviewing progress

The role of lead non-executive director involves:

  • meeting regularly with other non-executives

  • ensuring the Secretary of State is aware of any concerns

  • supporting the Secretary of State in his or her role as chair of the DBT Board

  • ensuring that the non-executives meet alone with the Secretary of State from time to time

  • liaising with the government-wide lead non-executive director

details.body:
HM Trade Commissioner for the Middle East and Pakistan cooperates closely with the wider diplomatic network and other colleagues to coordinate the government effort overseas to promote UK trade and prosperity.

The Trade Commissioner has full responsibility for all Department for Business and Trade (DBT) work in the region including:

  • growing the overall bilateral trade and investment relationship
  • improving market access for British companies, including small- and medium-sized businesses (SMEs)
  • developing finance and trade policy
  • UK Export Finance

ordered_roles.title: HM Trade Commissioner for the Middle East and Pakistan

details.body:
The HM Trade Commissioner (HMTC) for Europe leads a team of over 300 staff in 34 countries across the continent, working to encourage a thriving trade and investment relationship between the UK and other European countries.

The Trade Commissioner has responsibility for all Department for Business and Trade (DBT) work in Europe including:

  • improving market access for UK companies in Europe
  • attracting inward investment from European companies into UK
  • encouraging UK exports to Europe
  • influencing multilateral trade policy with Europe

The HMTC works closely with UK based government colleagues, UK ambassadors and the wider diplomatic network in Europe in delivering these goals. Together they engage extensively with UK and European businesses, European governments and wider stakeholders.

The HM Trade Commissioner for Africa works closely with the wider diplomatic network and other colleagues to coordinate the UK government effort to promote UK trade and investment across the African continent.

The Trade Commissioner has full responsibility for all Department for Business and Trade (DBT) work in Africa including:

  • growing the overall bilateral trade and investment relationship between the UK and Africa
  • improving market access for British companies, including small- and medium-sized businesses (SMEs)
  • developing finance and trade policy

The HM Trade Commissioner (HMTC) for Asia Pacific oversees all Department for Business and Trade (DBT) work in North-East Asia, South-East Asia and Australasia including:

  • growing the overall bilateral trade and investment relationship
  • improving market access for British companies, including small- and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs)
  • developing finance and trade policy

The HMTC also works closely with the wider diplomatic network and UK-based colleagues to coordinate His Majesty’s Government (HMG) activity on our wider prosperity agenda.

ordered_roles.title: HM Trade Commissioner for Asia Pacific

details.body:
The HM Trade Commissioner for Latin America and the Caribbean cooperates closely with the wider diplomatic network and other colleagues to coordinate the government effort overseas to promote UK trade and prosperity.

The Trade Commissioner has full responsibility for all Department for Business and Trade (DBT) work in Latin America and the Caribbean including:

  • growing the overall bilateral trade and investment relationship
  • improving market access for British companies, including small and medium sized businesses (SMEs)
  • developing trade policy

ordered_roles.title: HM Trade Commissioner for Latin America and the Caribbean

details.body:
The HM Trade Commissioner for Latin America and the Caribbean leads the UK’s Department for Business and Trade activity across Latin America and the Caribbean including:

  • strengthening trade and investment ties between the UK and the region
  • supporting UK companies to access and grow in Latin America and the Caribbean markets
  • advancing the UK’s trade policy and economic partnerships in the region

ordered_roles.title: HM Trade Commissioner for Latin America and the Caribbean

details.body:
The HM Trade Commissioner (HMTC) for Eastern Europe and Central Asia Region cooperates closely with the wider diplomatic network and other colleagues to coordinate the government effort overseas to promote UK trade and prosperity.

The Trade Commissioner has full responsibility for all Department for Business and Trade (DBT) work in Eastern Europe and Central Asia including:

  • growing the overall bilateral trade and investment relationship
  • improving market access for British companies, including small and medium sized businesses (SMEs)
  • developing finance and trade policy

ordered_roles.title: HM Trade Commissioner for Eastern Europe and Central Asia Region

details.body:
The HM Trade Commissioner (HMTC) for North America oversees all Department for Business and Trade (DBT) work in North America including:

  • growing the overall bilateral trade and investment relationship
  • improving market access for British companies, including small and medium sized businesses (SMEs)
  • developing finance and trade policy

The HMTC also works closely with the wider diplomatic network and UK based colleagues to coordinate HMG activity on our wider prosperity agenda.

ordered_roles.title: HM Trade Commissioner for North America

details.body: The Consul General is the senior UK official in a Consulate General, which is a subordinate office to the Embassy or High Commission, usually located in another major city. The Consul General represents the UK government and is typically responsible for consular and trade activities in their city or region.

ordered_roles.title: HM Consul General Milan and Director of Department for Business and Trade, Italy

details.body:

  • support on all free trade agreements (FTAs)
  • support on World Trade Organization (WTO), G7, G20, OECD, Commonwealth and wider multilateral engagement
  • market access strategy
  • Comprehensive and Progressive Agreement for Trans-Pacific Partnership (CPTPP) Bill
  • tariffs and disputes
  • UK’s crown dependencies and overseas territories
  • trade remedies
  • UK-EU Trade and Cooperation Agreement (TCA) implementation and review

  • the multilateral trading system
  • market access strategy
  • economic security
  • export controls and sanctions
  • responding to a changing international political landscape

ordered_roles.title: Director General, Economic Security and Trade Relations (ESTR)

details.body:

  • the multilateral trading system

ordered_roles.title: Director General, Economic Security and Trade Relations (ESTR)

details.body: The Country Director strengthens economic ties between the UK and Poland by promoting trade and investment, supporting UK businesses in Poland, and encouraging Polish investment in the UK. The role involves working with government, industry, and stakeholders to identify growth sectors, remove trade barriers, and foster a mutually beneficial business environment.

The Parliamentary Under Secretary of State (Minister for Women) is responsible for:

  • driving women’s policy and the government’s wider priorities for women and girls, including tackling violence against women and girls (with the Minister for Safeguarding)
  • tackling maternal and mental health disparities for minority groups
  • supporting improvements to the provision and accessibility of childcare to support working parents
  • progressing the Worker Protection Bill through Parliament
  • delivery of the government’s Inclusive Britain Action Plan
  • sponsorship of the Equality and Human Rights Commission

ordered_roles.title: Parliamentary Under Secretary of State (Minister for Women)

details.body:
The Parliamentary Under Secretary of State (Minister for Equalities) is responsible for:

  • advancing the government’s wider priorities for LGBT equality, including:
    • improving LGBT safety
    • supporting LGBT people fleeing persecution
    • tackling LGBT homelessness
  • preparing and progressing the Conversion Practices Bill through pre-legislative scrutiny and then through Parliament
  • progressing regulations to enable opposite-sex couples to convert their marriage to a civil partnership
  • sponsorship of the Social Mobility Commission, supporting work on geographic and socio-economic disparities
  • overview of the overarching equalities legislative framework, including the Equality Act

ordered_roles.title: Parliamentary Under Secretary of State (Minister for Equalities)

• Investment strategy – responsible for applying an investor lens to government policymaking and ensuring that investment-related decisions support the growth mission and the government strategies that underpin it
  • Investor relations

  • Office for Investment
  • Inward and foreign direct investment

The Director General for Industrial Strategy is responsible for:

  • overseeing the government’s industrial strategy to provide businesses with the long-term certainty they need to invest in sectors and places of highest growth potential, tackling barriers to increased investment and high-quality jobs, and ensuring tangible impact in communities right across the UK
  • leading the strategic centre of DBT, and the planning and coordination of work to drive sustainable growth, trade and the creation of good jobs, the department’s analysis group, the Chief Economist, the Office for the Chief Scientific Adviser and its global futures directorate

The Chief Strategic Business Adviser leads on significant business issues for the department including commercial opportunities and threats.
The Director General sits on the Department for Business and Trade departmental board and executive committee and helps deliver the Secretary of State’s and Minister for Exports’ export priorities.

The Head of the British Consulate-General is the most senior UK official in a consulate, which is an independent mission that feeds into an embassy or high commission, usually located in another major city. The Head of the Consulate-General represents the UK government and is typically responsible for consular and trade activities in their city or region.

The Director of the Department for Business and Trade in Germany works closely with the wider diplomatic network and other colleagues to coordinate the UK government effort to promote UK trade and investment and market access across the country.
